records.ety: [L. fabrica fabric, workshop: cf. F. fabrique fabric. See Forge.]

defs: The structure of anything; the manner in which the parts of a thing are united; workmanship; texture; make; as cloth of a beautiful fabric.
Cloth of any kind that is woven or knit from fibers, whether vegetable, animal, or synthetic; manufactured cloth; as, silks or other fabrics; made of a fabric that is 50% cotton and 50% polyester.
quotes: Anon out of the earth a fabric huge Rose like an exhalation.
Tithe was received by the bishop, . . . for the fabric of the churches for the poor.

defs: Any system or structure consisting of connected parts; as, the fabric of the universe.
quotes: The whole vast fabric of society.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fabricked (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fabricking.]

defs: To frame; to build; to construct.
